skill.name: typescript-review
skill.description: Review TypeScript and JavaScript code changes for Metabase coding standards, style issues, test expectations, and code quality concerns.
skill.summary: Review TypeScript and JavaScript pull requests against Metabase code quality standards.
skill.author: metabase

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are valid as command recommendations, but they are project-local Yarn review, lint, type-check, and test commands rather than hidden malware behavior. The weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive because the line only contains a natural-language skill description.

medium_findings.title: Project Script Execution Through Review Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run Yarn lint, formatting, type-check, and test commands. This is legitimate for TypeScript review, but it can execute project-defined scripts in an untrusted repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly listed and intended for execution during review. The risk is contextual because no malicious network, credential, or obfuscation behavior is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography hit occurs on the skill description line. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, cipher, or security-sensitive implementation is present there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is plain descriptive text about TypeScript and JavaScript code review. There is no evidence of cryptographic code or weak algorithm usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Commands in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes executable Yarn commands inside Markdown inline code. These commands are normal development tooling, but agents should only run them after inspecting the target repository context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command strings are directly visible in the skill. They are not malicious by themselves, but they create a real execution surface.

content.value_statement: TypeScript pull requests can miss project conventions, type-safety issues, and test gaps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused Metabase-style review checks.
actual_capabilities: Reviews TypeScript and JavaScript diffs for Metabase coding standards.
Checks for type-safety concerns and TypeScript usage problems.
Prompts use of ESLint, Prettier, type checking, and unit test commands.
Covers React best practices when changed code uses React.
Encourages review of JSDoc clarity and correctness.
limitations: The skill is tailored to Metabase project conventions.
It does not provide standalone static analysis logic.
It depends on repository Yarn scripts being present and trustworthy.
It does not define remediation steps for every possible review finding.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Review a TypeScript or JavaScript pull request for quality, style, and type-safety issues before merge.

use_cases.title: Pre-Submission Check
use_cases.description: Check local changes against Metabase review expectations before asking teammates for review.

use_cases.title: React Change Review
use_cases.description: Evaluate React-related changes for project conventions, clear documentation, and expected test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Diff
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of current TypeScript or JavaScript changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current TypeScript and JavaScript changes using the typescript-review skill. Focus on coding standards, type safety, and obvious test gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Check One File
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review of a specific changed file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typescript-review skill to review this file: [file path]. Check TypeScript usage, JSDoc quality, formatting concerns, and relevant tests.

prompt_templates.title: Review React Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typescript-review skill to review the React changes in this branch. Prioritize component patterns, state handling, type safety, and test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Full PR Gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the typescript-review skill as a full pull request gate. Inspect the diff, identify standards violations, recommend relevant Yarn checks, and separate blocking issues from suggestions.

output_examples.input: Review a TypeScript utility change before merge.
output: Blocking: one exported function accepts a broad type that hides invalid input.
Suggestion: add a focused unit test for the edge case introduced by the change.
Verification: run the project lint and type-check commands before merge.
Blocking: component state can become inconsistent when the prop changes.
Suggestion: clarify the JSDoc for the callback parameter.
Verification: run the matching unit test file and the TypeScript check.
No blocking style issues found in the reviewed diff.
Suggestion: keep import order consistent with nearby files.
Verification: run formatter and ESLint commands used by the repository.

best_practices: Inspect the diff and nearby code before running project scripts.
Report blocking correctness issues separately from style suggestions.
Verify findings with lint, type-check, and targeted tests when safe.
anti_patterns: Do not run project scripts from an untrusted repository without review.
Do not apply generic TypeScript rules that conflict with local conventions.
Do not treat formatting output as a substitute for code review.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews TypeScript and JavaScript changes for Metabase coding standards, type safety, documentation quality, tests, and React practices.

faq.question: Does it automatically fix code?
faq.answer: No. It guides review and suggests checks, but the agent or developer decides which fixes to apply.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill recommends running Yarn commands. Those commands are normal development checks, but project scripts can execute local code.

faq.question: Can I use it outside Metabase?
faq.answer: Yes, but its guidance reflects Metabase conventions. Other projects may need different lint, test, and style rules.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No network behavior is defined in the skill. It only lists local review and Yarn command workflows.
